MR051LWaterQualityNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for water quality monitoring. Tracks potable and technical water quality parameters aboard the International Space Station.Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/mr051l-water-quality#0.5.12
overviewMedical Requirements Overview0..1group
overview.mrid-numberMRID Number and Title0..1displayInitial Value: string = MR051L Water Quality Monitoring
overview.sponsorSponsor1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Sponsor Organizations#environmental-health "Environmental Health System"
overview.disciplineDiscipline1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#environmental "Environmental"
overview.purposePurpose/Objectives1..1textInitial Value: string = To monitor the quality of potable water, condensate, and technical water aboard ISS. Ensure water meets health and safety requirements for crew consumption and use.
overview.measurement-parametersMeasurement Parameters1..1stringInitial Value: string = Total organic carbon, pH, conductivity, microbial counts, iodine/silver biocide levels
overview.flight-durationFlight Duration1..1stringInitial Value: string = Continuous during ISS operations
preflight-trainingPreflight Training0..1group
preflight-training.descriptionTraining Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Environmental Health System training on water sampling procedures and equipment operation.
preflight-training.durationDuration (minutes)0..1integerInitial Value: integer = 60
preflight-training.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = L-12 months
preflight-training.completedTraining Completed0..1boolean
inflight-activitiesIn-Flight Activities0..1group
inflight-activities.descriptionActivity Description0..1textInitial Value: string = Regular water sampling and analysis using on-board equipment. Samples may be returned to ground for comprehensive analysis.
inflight-activities.scheduleSampling Schedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = Weekly for microbial, monthly for chemical parameters
inflight-activities.sample-locationSample Location0..*choiceOptions: 5 options

water-quality-results.phpH0..1decimal
water-quality-results.conductivityConductivity (uS/cm)0..1decimal

water-quality-results.total-coliformTotal Coliform (CFU/100mL)0..1integer
water-quality-results.heterotrophic-plate-countHeterotrophic Plate Count (CFU/mL)0..1integer
water-quality-results.iodine-levelIodine Level (mg/L)0..1decimal
water-quality-results.silver-levelSilver Level (mg/L)0..1decimal
water-quality-results.meets-specificationsMeets ISS Water Quality Specifications0..1boolean
water-quality-results.corrective-actionCorrective Action Required0..1boolean
water-quality-results.notesNotes0..1text

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MR051LWaterQuality
title: MR051L Water Quality Assessment
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for water quality monitoring. Tracks potable and technical water quality parameters aboard the International Space Station.
purpose: To monitor water quality aboard ISS to ensure crew health and safety. Includes potable water, condensate, and technical water systems.
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To monitor the quality of potable water, condensate, and technical water aboard ISS. Ensure water meets health and safety requirements for crew consumption and use.
Total organic carbon, pH, conductivity, microbial counts, iodine/silver biocide levels
Environmental Health System training on water sampling procedures and equipment operation.
Regular water sampling and analysis using on-board equipment. Samples may be returned to ground for comprehensive analysis.
Weekly for microbial, monthly for chemical parameters

code.display: Coliform bacteria [#/volume] in Water by Culture
